Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 | 名称 = Shirahige Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Shirahige Shrine (Ogaki, Gifu).JPG|280px|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{所在地}} | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Sunomata sirahige.JPG|250px|thumb|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しらひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sunomata, Sunomata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Arakata Shrine (Arakata Myōjin), a shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. The other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Sunomata-chō, Ōgaki)|Q11391073|WD=Q11391073|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Sunomata Hachiman Shrine}}. Its original location is said to have been on the riverbed of the current {{ill|lt=Sai River (Gifu)|Q7399814|WD=Q7399814|lt=Sai River|1=Sai River (Gifu)}}, on the boundary between {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}} and Anpachi District. === Overview === *It is located north of {{ill|lt=Sunomata Castle|Q1036473|WD=Q1036473|1=Sunomata Castle}}, and the precinct is part of Ichiyajō-ato Park. *There is Toyokuni Shrine as a precinct shrine. It was divided from {{ill|lt=Toyokuni Shrine (Osaka)|Q704855|WD=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Castle Park|Q150118|WD=Q150118|1=Osaka Castle Park}} when the imitation {{ill|lt=tenshu|Q49144|WD=Q49144|lt=castle tower|1=Tenshu}} ({{ill|lt=Ōgaki City Sunomata History Museum|Q115112030|WD=Q115112030|1=Sunomata Local History Museum}}) of Sunomata Castle was built. While the deities of Osaka’s Toyokuni Shrine are {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183|WD=Q1191183|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}, only Hideyoshi was enshrined in this branch shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === History === *The founding period is unknown. Books from the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} eras of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} mention “Shirahige Daimyōjin” and “Shirahige Shrine.” *{{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7): renamed Shirahige-sha. *{{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12): became a village shrine. *{{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13): renamed Shirahige Shrine. *{{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13): relocated to current site due to Sai River improvement works. *{{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 4): Toyokuni Shrine built as a precinct shrine. === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Gikaki Line of {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} **From stop 2 at {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal|Q11433817|WD=Q11433817|1=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal}} (south of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station|Q871579|WD=Q871579|1=Ōgaki Station}}), bound for {{ill|lt=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University|Q5560125|WD=Q5560125|1=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University}} or Gifu Ryūtsū Center South Gate *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Obusa-Sunomata line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Noriai Jidōsha|1=Gifu Bus}} **From stop 6 at {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|WD=Q11226229|1=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal}} (north of {{ill|lt=Gifu Station|Q1144885|WD=Q1144885|1=Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata **From stop 1 at {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area|Q11415771|WD=Q11415771|1=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area}} (west of {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|Q1109902|WD=Q1109902|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata === Notes === Books from the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} eras of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} mention “Shirahige Daimyōjin” and “Shirahige Shrine.” *{{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7): renamed Shirahige-sha. *{{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12): became a village shrine. *{{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13): renamed Shirahige Shrine. *{{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13): relocated to current site due to Sai River improvement works. *{{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{所在地}} | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Sunomata sirahige.JPG|250px|thumb|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しらひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sunomata, Sunomata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Arakata Shrine (Arakata Myōjin), a shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. The other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Sunomata-chō, Ōgaki)|Q11391073|WD=Q11391073|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Sunomata Hachiman Shrine}}. Its original location is said to have been on the riverbed of the current {{ill|lt=Sai River (Gifu)|Q7399814|WD=Q7399814|lt=Sai River|1=Sai River (Gifu)}}, on the boundary between {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}} and Anpachi District. === Overview === *It is located north of {{ill|lt=Sunomata Castle|Q1036473|WD=Q1036473|1=Sunomata Castle}}, and the precinct is part of Ichiyajō-ato Park. *There is Toyokuni Shrine as a precinct shrine. It was divided from {{ill|lt=Toyokuni Shrine (Osaka)|Q704855|WD=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Castle Park|Q150118|WD=Q150118|1=Osaka Castle Park}} when the imitation {{ill|lt=tenshu|Q49144|WD=Q49144|lt=castle tower|1=Tenshu}} ({{ill|lt=Ōgaki City Sunomata History Museum|Q115112030|WD=Q115112030|1=Sunomata Local History Museum}}) of Sunomata Castle was built. While the deities of Osaka’s Toyokuni Shrine are {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183|WD=Q1191183|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}, only Hideyoshi was enshrined in this branch shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === History === *The founding period is unknown. Books from the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} eras of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} mention “Shirahige Daimyōjin” and “Shirahige Shrine.” *{{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7): renamed Shirahige-sha. *{{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12): became a village shrine. *{{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13): renamed Shirahige Shrine. *{{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13): relocated to current site due to Sai River improvement works. *{{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 4): Toyokuni Shrine built as a precinct shrine. === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Gikaki Line of {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} **From stop 2 at {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal|Q11433817|WD=Q11433817|1=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal}} (south of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station|Q871579|WD=Q871579|1=Ōgaki Station}}), bound for {{ill|lt=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University|Q5560125|WD=Q5560125|1=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University}} or Gifu Ryūtsū Center South Gate *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Obusa-Sunomata line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Noriai Jidōsha|1=Gifu Bus}} **From stop 6 at {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 | 名称 = Shirahige Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Shirahige Shrine (Ogaki, Gifu).JPG|280px|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{所在地}} | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Sunomata sirahige.JPG|250px|thumb|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しらひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sunomata, Sunomata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Arakata Shrine (Arakata Myōjin), a shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. The other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Sunomata-chō, Ōgaki)|Q11391073|WD=Q11391073|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Sunomata Hachiman Shrine}}. Its original location is said to have been on the riverbed of the current {{ill|lt=Sai River (Gifu)|Q7399814|WD=Q7399814|lt=Sai River|1=Sai River (Gifu)}}, on the boundary between {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}} and Anpachi District. === Overview === *It is located north of {{ill|lt=Sunomata Castle|Q1036473|WD=Q1036473|1=Sunomata Castle}}, and the precinct is part of Ichiyajō-ato Park. *There is Toyokuni Shrine as a precinct shrine. It was divided from {{ill|lt=Toyokuni Shrine (Osaka)|Q704855|WD=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Castle Park|Q150118|WD=Q150118|1=Osaka Castle Park}} when the imitation {{ill|lt=tenshu|Q49144|WD=Q49144|lt=castle tower|1=Tenshu}} ({{ill|lt=Ōgaki City Sunomata History Museum|Q115112030|WD=Q115112030|1=Sunomata Local History Museum}}) of Sunomata Castle was built. While the deities of Osaka’s Toyokuni Shrine are {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183|WD=Q1191183|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}, only Hideyoshi was enshrined in this branch shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === History === *The founding period is unknown. Books from the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} eras of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} mention “Shirahige Daimyōjin” and “Shirahige Shrine.” *{{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7): renamed Shirahige-sha. *{{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12): became a village shrine. *{{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13): renamed Shirahige Shrine. *{{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13): relocated to current site due to Sai River improvement works. *{{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 4): Toyokuni Shrine built as a precinct shrine. === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Gikaki Line of {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} **From stop 2 at {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal|Q11433817|WD=Q11433817|1=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal}} (south of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station|Q871579|WD=Q871579|1=Ōgaki Station}}), bound for {{ill|lt=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University|Q5560125|WD=Q5560125|1=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University}} or Gifu Ryūtsū Center South Gate *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Obusa-Sunomata line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Noriai Jidōsha|1=Gifu Bus}} **From stop 6 at {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|WD=Q11226229|1=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal}} (north of {{ill|lt=Gifu Station|Q1144885|WD=Q1144885|1=Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata **From stop 1 at {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area|Q11415771|WD=Q11415771|1=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area}} (west of {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|Q1109902|WD=Q1109902|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata === Notes === Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 | 名称 = Shirahige Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Shirahige Shrine (Ogaki, Gifu).JPG|280px|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{所在地}} | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Sunomata sirahige.JPG|250px|thumb|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しらひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sunomata, Sunomata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Arakata Shrine (Arakata Myōjin), a shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. The other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Sunomata-chō, Ōgaki)|Q11391073|WD=Q11391073|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Sunomata Hachiman Shrine}}. Its original location is said to have been on the riverbed of the current {{ill|lt=Sai River (Gifu)|Q7399814|WD=Q7399814|lt=Sai River|1=Sai River (Gifu)}}, on the boundary between {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}} and Anpachi District. === Overview === *It is located north of {{ill|lt=Sunomata Castle|Q1036473|WD=Q1036473|1=Sunomata Castle}}, and the precinct is part of Ichiyajō-ato Park. *There is Toyokuni Shrine as a precinct shrine. It was divided from {{ill|lt=Toyokuni Shrine (Osaka)|Q704855|WD=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Castle Park|Q150118|WD=Q150118|1=Osaka Castle Park}} when the imitation {{ill|lt=tenshu|Q49144|WD=Q49144|lt=castle tower|1=Tenshu}} ({{ill|lt=Ōgaki City Sunomata History Museum|Q115112030|WD=Q115112030|1=Sunomata Local History Museum}}) of Sunomata Castle was built. While the deities of Osaka’s Toyokuni Shrine are {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183|WD=Q1191183|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}, only Hideyoshi was enshrined in this branch shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === History === *The founding period is unknown. Books from the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} eras of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} mention “Shirahige Daimyōjin” and “Shirahige Shrine.” *{{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7): renamed Shirahige-sha. *{{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12): became a village shrine. *{{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13): renamed Shirahige Shrine. *{{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13): relocated to current site due to Sai River improvement works. *{{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 4): Toyokuni Shrine built as a precinct shrine. === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Gikaki Line of {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} **From stop 2 at {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal|Q11433817|WD=Q11433817|1=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal}} (south of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station|Q871579|WD=Q871579|1=Ōgaki Station}}), bound for {{ill|lt=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University|Q5560125|WD=Q5560125|1=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University}} or Gifu Ryūtsū Center South Gate *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Obusa-Sunomata line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Noriai Jidōsha|1=Gifu Bus}} **From stop 6 at {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|WD=Q11226229|1=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal}} (north of {{ill|lt=Gifu Station|Q1144885|WD=Q1144885|1=Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata **From stop 1 at {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area|Q11415771|WD=Q11415771|1=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area}} (west of {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|Q1109902|WD=Q1109902|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata === Notes === Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年6月}} {{神社 | 名称 = Shirahige Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Shirahige Shrine (Ogaki, Gifu).JPG|280px|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{所在地}} | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | 創建 = Unknown | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Sunomata sirahige.JPG|250px|thumb|Sunomata-chō Shirahige Shrine]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しらひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sunomata, Sunomata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Arakata Shrine (Arakata Myōjin), a shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. The other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Sunomata-chō, Ōgaki)|Q11391073|WD=Q11391073|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Sunomata Hachiman Shrine}}. Its original location is said to have been on the riverbed of the current {{ill|lt=Sai River (Gifu)|Q7399814|WD=Q7399814|lt=Sai River|1=Sai River (Gifu)}}, on the boundary between {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}} and Anpachi District. === Overview === *It is located north of {{ill|lt=Sunomata Castle|Q1036473|WD=Q1036473|1=Sunomata Castle}}, and the precinct is part of Ichiyajō-ato Park. *There is Toyokuni Shrine as a precinct shrine. It was divided from {{ill|lt=Toyokuni Shrine (Osaka)|Q704855|WD=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Castle Park|Q150118|WD=Q150118|1=Osaka Castle Park}} when the imitation {{ill|lt=tenshu|Q49144|WD=Q49144|lt=castle tower|1=Tenshu}} ({{ill|lt=Ōgaki City Sunomata History Museum|Q115112030|WD=Q115112030|1=Sunomata Local History Museum}}) of Sunomata Castle was built. While the deities of Osaka’s Toyokuni Shrine are {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183|WD=Q1191183|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}, only Hideyoshi was enshrined in this branch shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === History === *The founding period is unknown. Books from the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} eras of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} mention “Shirahige Daimyōjin” and “Shirahige Shrine.” *{{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7): renamed Shirahige-sha. *{{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12): became a village shrine. *{{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13): renamed Shirahige Shrine. *{{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13): relocated to current site due to Sai River improvement works. *{{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 4): Toyokuni Shrine built as a precinct shrine. === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Gikaki Line of {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} **From stop 2 at {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal|Q11433817|WD=Q11433817|1=Ōgaki Station Bus Terminal}} (south of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Station|Q871579|WD=Q871579|1=Ōgaki Station}}), bound for {{ill|lt=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University|Q5560125|WD=Q5560125|1=Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University}} or Gifu Ryūtsū Center South Gate *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Obusa-Sunomata line of {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Noriai Jidōsha|1=Gifu Bus}} **From stop 6 at {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|WD=Q11226229|1=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal}} (north of {{ill|lt=Gifu Station|Q1144885|WD=Q1144885|1=Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata **From stop 1 at {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area|Q11415771|WD=Q11415771|1=Meitetsu Gifu Boarding Area}} (west of {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|Q1109902|WD=Q1109902|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}), bound for W65 Sunomata === Notes === The other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Sunomata-chō, Ōgaki)|Q11391073|WD=Q11391073|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Sunomata Hachiman Shrine}}. Its original location is said to have been on the riverbed of the current {{ill|lt=Sai River (Gifu)|Q7399814|WD=Q7399814|lt=Sai River|1=Sai River (Gifu)}}, on the boundary between {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}} and Anpachi District. === Overview === *It is located north of {{ill|lt=Sunomata Castle|Q1036473|WD=Q1036473|1=Sunomata Castle}}, and the precinct is part of Ichiyajō-ato Park. *There is Toyokuni Shrine as a precinct shrine. It was divided from {{ill|lt=Toyokuni Shrine (Osaka)|Q704855|WD=Q704855|lt=Toyokuni Shrine|1=Hōkoku Shrine (Osaka)}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Castle Park|Q150118|WD=Q150118|1=Osaka Castle Park}} when the imitation {{ill|lt=tenshu|Q49144|WD=Q49144|lt=castle tower|1=Tenshu}} ({{ill|lt=Ōgaki City Sunomata History Museum|Q115112030|WD=Q115112030|1=Sunomata Local History Museum}}) of Sunomata Castle was built. While the deities of Osaka’s Toyokuni Shrine are {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}, and {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183|WD=Q1191183|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}, only Hideyoshi was enshrined in this branch shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === History === *The founding period is unknown. Books from the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} eras of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} mention “Shirahige Daimyōjin” and “Shirahige Shrine.” *{{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7): renamed Shirahige-sha. *{{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12): became a village shrine. *{{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13): renamed Shirahige Shrine. *{{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13): relocated to current site due to Sai River improvement works. *{{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 4): Toyokuni Shrine built as a precinct shrine. === Transportation === *15 minutes on foot from “Sunomata” bus stop on the Gikaki Line of {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu
